title: Heatwave conditions likely to intensify across India, IMD issues alert - News9live
full_text:

Heatwave conditions likely to intensify across India, IMD issues alert
India weather: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has predicted that temperatures across the country will rise as summer starts to set in. The weather agency has issued a heatwave alert for isolated pockets of Chhattisgarh, Odisha, and east Madhya Pradesh.
IMD has already announced alerts for above-normal maximum temperatures across most of the country from March to May 2026.
New Delhi: Temperatures across north India will move upwards in the coming days after most parts of the region witnessed pleasant weather throughout March and early April.
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has predicted that temperatures across the country will rise as summer starts to set in. The weather agency has issued a heatwave alert for isolated pockets of Chhattisgarh, Odisha, and east Madhya Pradesh. In addition, hot and humid conditions are expected to prevail across several regions, including West Bengal, Jharkhand, Odisha, and the Gujarat region, along with Saurashtra and Kutch. Similar conditions are likely over Rayalaseema, coastal Andhra Pradesh, and parts of southern India, including coastal Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, Puducherry, and Karaikal.
Above-normal temperature across India
IMD has already announced alerts for above-normal maximum temperatures across most of the country from March to May 2026.
Rainfall is also likely to persist in some parts of the country amid rising temperatures. Northeast India is set to experience fairly widespread light to modest rain accompanied by thunderstorms, lightning, and gusty winds, with heavy rainfall forecasted over Arunachal Pradesh which could lead to localized disruptions. In eastern parts of the country Including West Bengal, Sikkim and Odisha scattered showers accompanied by thunderstorms and gusty winds. Southern states such as Karnataka, Andhra Pradesh, and Kerala, may witness isolated rainfall with thunderstorms and lightning. While the remaining parts of the country are likely to remain dry with no major weather activity anticipated and heat continuing to dominate conditions.
What IMD predicts for northwest India
The northwest India is likely to see an increase in maximum temperatures, while central and eastern regions may witness a gradual rise before conditions stabilise. Western state such as Gujrat and Maharashtra are expected to go through mild changes.
In Delhi, temperature is set to rise with maximum temperatures expected to hover between 35–37°C, while the minimum temperatures are likely to stay in the range 18 to 20°C on Monday. IMD has also predicted the temperature to cross 40°C later in the week.

title: Severe Heatwave Grips Odisha; Temperatures Cross 40°C Across Several Cities - businessnewsthisweek.com
full_text:
Bhubaneswar, Apr 13 (BNP): Odisha is reeling under an intense heatwave coupled with high humidity, with temperatures soaring above 40°C in several parts of the state, making conditions extremely uncomfortable for residents.
According to weather reports, multiple cities have recorded significantly high daytime temperatures, while rising humidity levels have further aggravated the discomfort, leading to sweltering conditions even during evening hours.
The prevailing heatwave is expected to continue over the next few days, prompting authorities to issue advisories urging people to avoid direct sun exposure during peak hours and stay hydrated.
Health experts have warned of an increased risk of heat-related illnesses such as dehydration, heat exhaustion, and heatstroke, particularly among children, the elderly, and outdoor workers.
Meanwhile, the administration is closely monitoring the situation and has advised necessary precautions to minimize health risks amid the ongoing heatwave conditions.
